title: Map Tenant Slug/Subdomain to Stytch Organization
description:
Establish a canonical mapping between tenant subdomains (e.g., `toyota.datatoolspro.com`) and their Stytch Organizations so that login can be initiated deterministically based on the URL.

## Requirements

- Tenant directory stores `tenant_slug` → `stytch_organization_id` mapping
- Lookup is fast (<50ms typical, via edge cache or KV)
- Unknown tenant slugs return a safe error page with no auth information leakage

## Open Questions

- Will every tenant have a separate subdomain (e.g., `toyota.datatoolspro.com`)?
- Should there also be a shared login domain alongside tenant-specific subdomains?
- For SSO customers, should login be strictly locked to the tenant's Stytch Organization based on subdomain with no fallback?
- Should tenant resolution also determine regional routing (EU/US/APAC)?
acceptance_criteria: Tenant directory maps tenant_slug to stytch_organization_id with fast lookup
Lookup latency is under 50ms via edge cache or KV
Unknown tenant slugs return a safe error page without leaking auth details
Mapping is deterministic — same slug always resolves to same Organization
middleware_changes: Tenant directory lookup service that resolves tenant_slug to stytch_organization_id. Includes edge caching layer for sub-50ms response times.
data_model_changes: Tenant directory data structure storing slug-to-organization mappings, potentially in Cloudflare KV or database with cache.
implementation_notes: Key architectural decisions pending: subdomain strategy (per-tenant subdomains vs shared login domain), SSO strictness policy, and regional routing integration. These decisions affect the tenant directory schema and lookup logic.
comment_summary: Developer asked for clarification on subdomain strategy: whether every tenant gets a unique subdomain, whether there's a shared login domain fallback, whether SSO should be strictly scoped to subdomain, and whether tenant resolution should include regional routing.
